# FinalitySignature

Fires when a validator submits a finality signature for a block. A block achieves strict finality once validators controlling more than two-thirds of total stake weight have submitted signatures - at that point the block is irreversible.

## Import

```ts
import { SseClient, EventName } from 'casper-js-sdk';
```

## Usage

```ts
sseClient.subscribe(EventName.FinalitySignatureEventType, (raw) => {
  const result = raw.parseAsFinalitySignatureEvent();
  if (result.err) return;

  const sig = result.val.finalitySignature;

  // Casper 2.x
  const v2 = sig.getFinalitySignatureV2();
  if (v2) {
    console.log('Block hash:', v2.blockHash);
    console.log('Validator:', v2.publicKey.toHex());
    console.log('Chain name hash:', v2.chainNameHash);
    console.log('Signature:', v2.signature);
  }

  // Casper 1.x
  const v1 = sig.getFinalitySignatureV1();
  if (v1) {
    console.log('Block hash:', v1.blockHash);
    console.log('Validator:', v1.publicKey.toHex());
  }
});
```

## Payload

The `FinalitySignature` wrapper holds either a V1 or V2 signature.

| Method | Returns |
|---|---|
| `getFinalitySignatureV2()` | `FinalitySignatureV2 \| undefined` |
| `getFinalitySignatureV1()` | `FinalitySignatureV1 \| undefined` |

### FinalitySignatureV2 Fields

| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `blockHash` | `string` | Signed block hash |
| `blockHeight` | `number` | Block height |
| `chainNameHash` | `string` | Hash of the chain name |
| `era` | `number` | Era ID |
| `publicKey` | `PublicKey` | Signing validator's key |
| `signature` | `string` | Hex-encoded signature |
